rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the paper's thesis, contribution framing, and whether the stated deliverable coheres with the claimed method contribution.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the artifact centrally involves checking whether claims such as being fully unsupervised are logically consistent with the described calibration procedure.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation centrally calls for judging whether the reported experiments and results support the framework, guarantees, benchmark, and performance claims made in the draft.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad request must disclose uncertainty because the intended type of feedback is underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims of priority, guarantees, and unsupervised status, so avoiding overclaiming is an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection rather than actually critiquing, rewriting, or validating the paper.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for potential downstream harm from overstated scientific claims or unsupported benchmark assertions.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability is needed so each responsibility assignment is tied to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
